message:
fix(policy): improve DCG subprocess error handling and robustness

The run_dcg function had several reliability issues:

1. stderr was discarded (Stdio::null) making it impossible to diagnose
   DCG failures. Now captured via Stdio::piped().

2. stdin was borrowed via as_mut() which kept the borrow alive and could
   cause the pipe to remain open, preventing the child from seeing EOF.
   Changed to stdin.take() which drops the handle after writing, properly
   signaling end-of-input.

3. Non-zero exit codes from DCG were silently ignored, potentially
   treating a crashed DCG process as "allow". Now explicitly checks
   output.status.success() and returns DcgError::Failed with the most
   informative available output (stderr preferred, then stdout).

4. Permission decision comparison was case-sensitive ("deny" only),
   which could miss "Deny" or "DENY" responses from DCG. Changed to
   eq_ignore_ascii_case for defensive parsing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
